title: Dielytra cucullaria
taxonomy: Dielytra cucullaria is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Dieluraceae. It is a small, annual or biennial herb with distinctive flowers and leaves. Dielytra cucullaria is native to North America and is often found in damp to wet environments. The plant has been placed in the family Dieluraceae, which is a small family of flowering plants. However, some sources may group Dielytra cucullaria in the family Phrymaceae.
uses: Dielytra cucullaria, commonly known as the umbrella plant, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and stems are used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In some cultures, the plant is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
